_comment: CLAP backend — S3 lifecycle configuration (G1).
Rationale:
Audio recordings and PDF reports accumulate indefinitely without a lifecycle
policy. At 6,000 students × ~10 submissions × ~5 MB audio + ~0.5 MB PDF =
~330 GB over one academic year. After the retention period, objects should
be tiered to cheaper storage then expired to contain cost.
Instructions (AWS Console):
1. S3 Console → Bucket → Management → Lifecycle Rules → Create Rule
2. Apply the two rules below (one for audio/, one for reports/).
3. Confirm tiering and expiry dates with institutional data-retention policy.
Instructions (AWS CLI):
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
--bucket BUCKET_NAME \
--lifecycle-configuration file://s3-lifecycle.json
(strip _comment keys first, or use the Rules array directly)
Instructions (Supabase Storage):
Supabase Storage does not yet support S3-compatible lifecycle rules via API.
Implement a monthly Celery task that calls delete_from_storage() for objects
older than the retention threshold (query submission_score.created_at).
Alternatively, migrate to AWS S3 where lifecycle is natively supported.

Transitions._comment: Move to Infrequent Access after 90 days — ~46% cost reduction
Move to Glacier Instant Retrieval after 6 months — ~68% cost reduction

Expiration._comment: Delete audio after 1 year. Adjust to institutional data-retention policy.

Transitions._comment: Move reports to Infrequent Access after 1 year

Expiration._comment: Delete reports after 3 years. Adjust to institutional retention policy.
